overview.description: A single-user desktop application (Electron) that unifies four productivity tools into one coherent daily workflow: task management, time-boxed focus sessions (Pomodoro), automatic calendar logging, and end-of-day journaling.
overview.core_philosophy: Minimum input, maximum insight. The app learns your day from your work — not by asking you to log it. The calendar fills itself. The journal pre-populates with facts. You only add the narrative.

goals: Provide a unified daily loop: Plan → Execute → Track → Reflect
Auto-generate calendar blocks from completed Pomodoro sessions (no manual input)
Pre-populate journal entries with the day's data to reduce friction
Store all data locally with SQLite — no internet dependency
Ship a premium, polished desktop experience via Electron
non_goals_v1: No cloud sync or multi-device support
No external calendar integrations (Google Calendar, etc.)
No week view calendar (deferred to v2)
No past journal browser (deferred to v2)

user.name: Tang Jin Hang
user.description: Works on complex, cognitively demanding tasks. Wants to be intentional about how time is spent and have a clear end-of-day record without manually logging everything.

morning.label: Morning
steps: Review carry-over tasks via inline banner

during_day.label: During the Day
steps: Pick a task and start a Pomodoro
Persistent header bar shows active timer and progress bar
Rest of UI dims and collapses during focus
On session end: mark outcome (Done / Still going / Blocked)

evening.label: Evening
steps: Auto-summary pre-fills: tasks completed, total focus time, calendar blocks
User writes narrative on top in a markdown editor

theme.aesthetic: Neutral and minimal. Premium without being flashy. Carefully balanced whitespace, refined typography, and subtle depth. The kind of UI that makes you pause when you first open it.

color_palette.base: Neutral near-white (light mode) / near-black (dark mode) with subtle warm or cool undertones
color_palette.accent: Single hue used sparingly for interactive elements and active states
color_palette.category_colors: Distinct but desaturated — readable, not loud
color_palette.gradients: Only where they add depth (e.g., progress bar fill)

theme.typography: Clean sans-serif with intentional weight hierarchy. Body text comfortable for long sessions.
theme.micro_interactions: Subtle transitions on hover, focus, and state changes. Responsive and alive without being distracting.

navigation.behavior_during_pomodoro: Sidebar remains fully accessible and is NOT greyed out during a session

contents: Session progress bar (fills across work duration)
pomodoro_header_bar.focus_mode_effect: Content below header dims and collapses — task list is faded and non-interactive; only header bar is fully lit and actionable

carry_over.trigger: Start of a new day with incomplete tasks from the previous day
carry_over.ui: Inline banner at the top of Today's Board
carry_over.dismissal: Banner dismissed once all tasks are resolved

todo_list.rule: Tasks are always linked to a Pomodoro session — no floating sessions

during_focus.header_bar: Shows task name, countdown, progress bar

on_session_end.blocked_flow: Optional one-liner: 'What's blocking you?' saved to block_note

pomodoro_timer.timer_implementation: Web Worker (prevents drift when window is minimized)

calendar.source: Every completed Pomodoro session creates one time block automatically

user_narrative.implementation: CodeMirror 6 with markdown mode and custom app theme
features: Live preview (WYSIWYG markdown rendering as you type)
Keyboard shortcuts for formatting (bold, italic, headings, lists, checkboxes)

carry_over_date.purpose: Date the task was carried forward to

color_tag.note: Category color at time of session

CalendarBlock.rule: Always derived from PomodoroSession — never created directly

auto_summary.note: Recomputed on journal open from session data for that date

tech_stack.reason: Fast for desktop, no SSR needed
Lightweight, good for timer + task state
Prevents drift when window is minimized

ux_principles: One primary action at a time — planning, focusing, or reflecting; never all three at once
The calendar is earned, not built — blocks come from real work, making it motivating
Minimum input, maximum insight — the app learns your day; you don't log it
Friction-free journal — the hard part (recalling what you did) is already done for you
Focus mode collapses, not hides — the UI dims below the header so context is preserved but distraction is eliminated
Premium feel, no clutter — every element earns its place; whitespace is intentional

screens.name: Today's Board
screens.description: Task list + active Pomodoro header. Primary daily view. Carry-over banner shown at top when applicable.

screens.name: Calendar — Day View
screens.description: All logged time blocks for a selected date, color-coded by category. Read-only.

screens.name: Journal
screens.description: Today's pre-populated summary + Obsidian-style markdown narrative editor.

screens.name: Settings
screens.description: Timer durations, sound preferences, category management, theme toggle, start-on-login.

settings.label: Categories
settings.description: Add, rename, recolor, or delete user-defined categories. Predefined categories (Work, Personal, Learning) can be recolored but not deleted.

v1_in_scope: Full todo list with prioritization, drag-and-drop, and carry-over banner
Pomodoro timer linked to tasks with outcome tracking and Web Worker accuracy
Daily journal with auto-populated summary and Obsidian-style markdown editor
Settings: timer durations, sound, start-on-login, category management
deferred_v2: AI-generated summaries and pattern detection (Anthropic SDK)

success_metrics: Daily active use for 30 consecutive days
Calendar accurately reflects actual work time with zero manual input
Journal entries feel useful for reflection, not a chore
Timer drift < 1 second over a 25-minute session
Opening the app feels good — the UI quality is immediately apparent
